statement: A compact metric space is separable.
lean_statement:
theorem separableSpace_of_compact_metric
    {X : Type*} [MetricSpace X] [CompactSpace X] :
    TopologicalSpace.SeparableSpace X
proof:
Proof: for each `n ≥ 1`, cover `X` by the open balls of radius `1/n`. By compactness, there is a
finite subcover; let `F_n` be the finite set of chosen centers. Then `D = ⋃_n F_n` is countable.
We claim `D` is dense: given any nonempty open set `U`, pick `x ∈ U` and choose `n` such that the
ball `B(x, 1/n)` is contained in `U`. Since `F_n` covers `X` with radius `1/n` balls, some
`y ∈ F_n` lies in `B(x, 1/n) ⊆ U`. Hence every nonempty open set meets `D`, so `X` is separable.
